description: Regression fixture for issue #501: a native app surface (whose preflight JIT-installs the NovaWindows driver mid-run on a fresh runner) followed by a ConPTY `tty` background process in the same runner process. Before the fix, that mid-run npm install pruned node-pty's files from the runtime cache (`--no-save` against a dependency-less runtime manifest made npm treat every sibling as extraneous), and the next pty.spawn — served from the stale module caches — froze the whole process; in a fresh process it silently SKIPped instead. With the non-destructive-install fix and the on-disk self-heal gate, this pairing must PASS: the tty step starts, echoes through the PTY, and closes. Lives in the apps group so the driver-install/tty interleaving actually occurs in one process; the tty half runs on every platform leg, the app half is Windows-gated.

tests.description: Open and close a real app surface first, so the app session lifecycle (driver JIT install, per-context Appium server, teardown tree-kill) runs before any PTY work.
Start node -i under a PTY after the app test, type an expression, wait for the echoed result, close. Must never freeze; SKIPs only when node-pty is genuinely unavailable on the platform.
